trivia_bullets.text: Theros Beyond Death (released January 2020) returns to the Greek-mythology-inspired plane of Theros, with a central narrative focused on Elspeth Tirel escaping the Underworld and defying the gods. The set's thematic spine is mortal defiance against divine authority, which the flavor text here embodies directly.
sources: Scryfall: set Theros Beyond Death, released 2020-01-24

trivia_bullets.text: Revoke Existence originated in Scars of Mirrodin (October 2010, art by Allen Williams, flavor text by Ganedor, loxodon mystic). First reprinted Born of the Gods (February 2014) with Uremides quote; then Duel Decks: Venser vs. Koth; then Double Masters (August 2020). The THB printing uses the same Adam Paquette art and Tikaia flavor text as the Double Masters reprint — this is the fourth unique print of the card.

"The time has come for us to throw off the chains of tyrannical gods and antiquated superstitions." — Tikaia, disciple of Uremides. Uremides is a philosopher of the polis Meletis; the Uremidean school emphasizes logical reasoning and is explicitly skeptical of divine authority. The Born of the Gods printing quoted Uremides directly: 'There will come a time when the gods will look at mortals and shudder.' The THB reprint quotes his disciple, extending the same lineage.
sources: MTG Wiki: Meletis, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Revoke Existence is a Sorcery that exiles a single artifact or enchantment for 2 mana. Legal in Pauper, Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, and Commander. The exile clause is particularly relevant on Theros where gods are enchantment-typed and indestructible — destroy effects fail, exile does not.
